destination.name: Gargantua
destination.description: A rapidly spinning supermassive black hole with a glowing accretion disk.

destination.name: Cygnus X-1
destination.description: A galactic X-ray source and one of the first proven black holes.

destination.name: Sagittarius A*
destination.description: The supermassive black hole at the very center of the Milky Way galaxy.

destination.name: TON 618
destination.description: An ultramassive black hole, one of the largest known in the universe.

destination.name: V616 Monocerotis
destination.description: One of the closest known black holes to our solar system.

destination.name: M87*
destination.description: The first black hole ever directly imaged by humanity.

destination.name: The Black Hole
destination.description: A mysterious anomaly surrounded by intense gravitational waves.

destination.name: Cygnus A
destination.description: A supermassive black hole fueling an incredibly bright radio galaxy.

destination.name: Great Attractor
destination.description: A massive gravitational anomaly drawing in millions of galaxies.

destination.name: Apex Black Hole
destination.description: A highly unstable singularity near the edge of the known universe.

destination.name: Void-9
destination.description: A pitch-black region of space hiding a dormant singularity.

destination.name: Singularity-X
destination.description: A theoretical black hole emitting strange temporal distortions.

destination.name: PSR J1614-2230
destination.description: A massive neutron star on the verge of collapsing into a black hole.

destination.name: GS 2023+338
destination.description: A well-known binary black hole system.

destination.name: Perseus Cluster BH
destination.description: Famous for emitting acoustic waves that create a cosmic B-flat note.

destination.name: Pandora
destination.description: A lush, biologically diverse moon orbiting a gas giant, rich in floating mountains.

destination.name: Miller's Planet
destination.description: A water-world orbiting dangerously close to a supermassive black hole.

destination.name: Tatooine
destination.description: A harsh desert world orbiting twin suns, home to moisture farmers.

destination.name: Mann’s Planet
destination.description: An icy, treacherous world with frozen clouds and ammonia atmosphere.

destination.name: Edmunds’ Planet
destination.description: A promising desert world with breathable air and a stable ecosystem.

destination.name: Arrakis
destination.description: A dangerous desert planet and the only known source of the spice melange.

destination.name: Proxima Centauri b
destination.description: An Earth-sized exoplanet orbiting the closest star to the solar system.

destination.name: Kepler-186f
destination.description: The first Earth-sized planet found in the habitable zone of another star.

destination.name: TRAPPIST-1e
destination.description: A potentially habitable rocky planet in a highly compact planetary system.

destination.name: LV-426
destination.description: A dark, stormy terraformed moon with a history of hostile encounters.

destination.name: Naboo
destination.description: A beautiful, peaceful world with sprawling plains and underwater cities.

destination.name: Magrathea
destination.description: A legendary planet known for its custom planet-building industry.

destination.name: Reach
destination.description: A major military stronghold planet with a diverse, Earth-like climate.

destination.name: Gliese 581g
destination.description: A highly debated exoplanet thought to sit perfectly in its star's habitable zone.

destination.name: Zion
destination.description: A hidden, subterranean bastion of human civilization.

destination.name: Ego the Living Planet
destination.description: A sentient, celestial entity manifesting as an entire planetary mass.

destination.name: Hoth
destination.description: A desolate world covered in snow and ice, entirely hostile to unprotected life.

destination.name: Coruscant
destination.description: An ecumenopolis completely covered by urban sprawl.

destination.name: Dagobah
destination.description: A swamp-covered planet with massive biological diversity but highly toxic areas.

destination.name: Geonosis
destination.description: A rocky, barren desert world featuring massive droid foundries.

destination.name: Utapau
destination.description: A sinkhole-riddled planet composed mostly of porous rock and deep caverns.

destination.name: Alderaan (Remains)
destination.description: An asteroid field that was once a peaceful, mountainous world.

destination.name: Krypton (Remains)
destination.description: A highly radioactive debris field of a former advanced civilization.

destination.name: Apokolips
destination.description: A dark, fiery ecumenopolis dominated by massive fire pits.

destination.name: Mesklin
destination.description: A massive, disc-shaped planet with bone-crushing gravity at its poles.

destination.name: Solaris
destination.description: A world completely covered by a mysterious, seemingly sentient ocean.

destination.name: Mongo
destination.description: A rogue planet with a highly volatile atmosphere and environment.

destination.name: Bizarro World
destination.description: A cube-shaped planet where physics behave inversely.

destination.name: Vulcan (Destroyed)
destination.description: A former red, arid planet, now a micro-singularity hazard zone.

destination.name: Phaëton
destination.description: A theoretical destroyed planet between Mars and Jupiter.

destination.name: Titan
destination.description: Saturn's largest moon, featuring a dense atmosphere and liquid methane lakes.

destination.name: Pluto
destination.description: A distant, icy dwarf planet on the very edge of our solar system.

destination.name: Europa
destination.description: Jupiter's moon, hiding a massive liquid water ocean beneath its icy crust.

destination.name: Thebe
destination.description: A small, red, irregularly shaped inner moon of Jupiter.

destination.name: Metis
destination.description: Jupiter's innermost moon, orbiting within the planet's faint main ring.

destination.name: Io
destination.description: The most volcanically active body in the solar system, orbiting Jupiter.

destination.name: Ganymede
destination.description: The largest moon in the solar system, complete with its own magnetic field.

destination.name: Callisto
destination.description: Jupiter's heavily cratered moon, holding the record for most impact scars.

destination.name: Enceladus
destination.description: A tiny, icy moon of Saturn famous for its massive water geysers.

destination.name: Triton
destination.description: Neptune's largest moon, featuring a retrograde orbit and ice volcanoes.

destination.name: Charon
destination.description: Pluto's massive companion moon, locked in a binary dance.

destination.name: Phobos
destination.description: One of Mars' tiny, doomed moons, slowly spiraling inward toward the planet.

destination.name: Deimos
destination.description: The smaller, outermost, and lumpy-shaped moon of Mars.

destination.name: Mimas
destination.description: Saturn's moon, featuring a massive crater taking up a third of its face.

destination.name: Iapetus
destination.description: Saturn's two-toned moon, complete with a massive equatorial ridge.

destination.name: Nidavellir
destination.description: A dying neutron star harnessed by a legendary cosmic forge.

destination.name: Proxima Centauri
destination.description: The closest star to the solar system, a small, low-mass red dwarf.

destination.name: Sirius A
destination.description: The brightest star in Earth's night sky, a massive main-sequence star.

destination.name: Epsilon Eridani
destination.description: A young, active sun-like star with two distinct asteroid belts.

destination.name: Tau Ceti
destination.description: A stable, solitary star, a frequent setting for early sci-fi exploration.

destination.name: Luyten’s Star
destination.description: A quiet red dwarf known for its potentially habitable exoplanets.

destination.name: Wolf 1061
destination.description: A red dwarf system heavily studied for colonial expansion.

destination.name: Gliese 667C
destination.description: Part of a triple-star system, bathing its planets in an eerie red light.

destination.name: Vega
destination.description: A brilliant, fast-spinning blue star, the first star to be photographed.

destination.name: TRAPPIST-1
destination.description: An ultra-cool red dwarf hosting a record seven Earth-sized planets.

destination.name: Alpha Centauri A
destination.description: A sun-like star forming a binary pair with Alpha Centauri B.

destination.name: Alpha Centauri B
destination.description: The orange-hued companion to Alpha Centauri A.

destination.name: Procyon A
destination.description: A bright, white star, nearing the end of its main-sequence life.

destination.name: Altair
destination.description: A massive star spinning so fast it is flattened at the poles.

destination.name: Fomalhaut A
destination.description: A young, bright star surrounded by a massive, glowing dust ring.

destination.name: Pillars of Creation
destination.description: Massive columns of interstellar dust and gas, serving as a stellar nursery.

destination.name: Orion Nebula
destination.description: A vibrant, glowing cloud of cosmic debris visible to the naked eye from Earth.

destination.name: Crab Nebula
destination.description: A sprawling supernova remnant acting as a pulsar wind nebula.

destination.name: Eagle Nebula
destination.description: An open cluster of stars set against a magnificent backdrop of gas.

destination.name: Omega Nebula
destination.description: A bright, glowing emission nebula that looks like a majestic swan.

destination.name: Trifid Nebula
destination.description: A complex cosmic cloud displaying three distinct types of nebulas in one.

destination.name: Lagoon Nebula
destination.description: A giant interstellar cloud heavily populated with new, massive stars.

destination.name: Ring Nebula
destination.description: A glowing shell of ionized gas expelled by a dying, sun-like star.

destination.name: Hourglass Nebula
destination.description: A young planetary nebula featuring two massive rings that look like an eye.

destination.name: Snowball Nebula
destination.description: A planetary nebula sporting a distinctive icy-blue outer shell.

destination.name: Helix Nebula
destination.description: Often called the Eye of God, it is one of the closest planetary nebulas to Earth.

destination.name: Horsehead Nebula
destination.description: A famous dark nebula set against the bright emission of IC 434.

destination.name: Cat's Eye Nebula
destination.description: One of the most complex, structurally intricate nebulas ever discovered.

destination.name: Butterfly Nebula
destination.description: A dying star casting off waves of super-heated gas resembling glowing wings.

destination.name: Veil Nebula
destination.description: A massive, delicate, and wispy remnant of a star that exploded 8,000 years ago.

package.description: A chaotic, high-thrill journey across legendary cosmic locations.
Follow in the footsteps of humanity's greatest explorers.
Travel the ancient hyperspace lanes to iconic planetary systems.
Journey to the edge of known exoplanets and distant voids.
A high-thrill tour skirting the edge of stellar phenomena.
Explore treacherous environments and breathtaking cosmic events.
Focuses entirely on the most visually stunning locations in the galaxy.
A tight orbit tour focusing on close-range, high-profile anomalies.
Tours of potential human-habitable worlds and their surrounding systems.
A deep dive bridging outer planet outposts with extreme deep space.
A physics-defying route focusing on gravitational extremes.
A luxury sweep combining historical planetary ruins with stellar beauty.
Bask in the light of diverse stars before diving into the absolute dark.
Venture into the dangerous fringes of the known galaxy.
A long-range tour covering one of every extreme category in existence.
